messages: What's the most surprising fact about honey you remember? I heard it lasts forever—is that true?
Back to the buying dilemma: I'm torn between raw rendering power and portability—given how I described my workflow and daily routine, which trade-off would you expect to make more sense?
For on-the-go file work, would it normally be better to invest in very fast external drives now or insist on larger internal storage when I purchase?
From a budgeting angle, if I can cover a chunk upfront and use the short store installment for the rest, is that a reasonable way to manage irregular freelance income?
If my goal is to avoid needing another upgrade for at least a couple of worry-free years, does that push the decision toward spending more now or choosing the more efficient newer option?
Putting everything together, if you had to pick one practical compromise for someone in my situation, which would you choose and why?
